title: The One With The Giant Poking Device

expressions.dialogue:
Rachel: Joey, you can't **keep this to yourself**, if you know about this, you have to tell him.
Joey: It'll kill him. I mean it'll, it'll just kill him.
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.nuance: 누군가에게 비밀을 지키도록 요청하거나 지키지 말라고 충고할 때 사용. 'You can't keep this to yourself'처럼 부정형으로 쓰이면 '혼자만 알고 있으면 안 된다'는 의미가 강해진다.
expressions.example: I know about the surprise party, but I'm keeping it to myself. / 나는 깜짝 파티에 대해 알고 있지만 아무에게도 말 안 할 거야.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Yeah. Listen, can you keep this information to yourself?

expressions.dialogue:
Rachel: Joey, you can't keep this to yourself, if you know about this, you have to tell him.
Joey: **It'll kill him.** I mean it'll, it'll just kill him.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: (비유적으로) 그에게 엄청난 충격이나 상처를 줄 것이다
expressions.nuance: 실제 죽음이 아니라 감정적 충격이나 고통을 과장해서 표현할 때 쓴다. 'That would kill me'처럼 1인칭으로도 자주 쓰이며, 매우 구어적인 표현이다.
expressions.example: Don't tell him she got promoted — it'll kill him. / 그녀가 승진했다는 말은 하지 마 — 엄청난 충격을 받을 거야.

expressions.dialogue:
Ross: Joey, Homo Sapiens are people.
Joey: Hey-hey, **I'm not judging.**
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 판단하거나 비판하는 게 아니에요 (그냥 말하는 것뿐)
expressions.nuance: 자신의 발언이 비판적으로 들릴 수 있을 때 면책용으로 쓰는 표현. 유머러스한 상황에서 자주 등장하며, 황당한 말을 한 뒤 뻔뻔하게 쓰이기도 한다.
expressions.example: You ate the whole pizza by yourself? I'm not judging, just impressed. / 혼자 피자 다 먹었어? 판단하는 게 아니라, 그냥 대단하다고.

occ.speaker: Mike
occ.text: Oh she's my girlfriend. That's not just how we do it here. I got to get a break and when we come back we've got Kenneth singing "I touch myself"...I'm not here to judge!

expressions.dialogue: Chandler: Y'know **it serves me right** for buying that twelve pack of condoms. And now I can't even return them, because she choked on the receipt!
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.nuance: 자신의 행동이 불러온 나쁜 결과를 스스로 인정할 때 쓴다. 'Serves you right'라고 하면 '꼴좋다, 당연하지'라는 뜻으로 상대방에게도 쓸 수 있다.
expressions.example: I didn't study and I failed. Serves me right. / 공부 안 했더니 떨어졌네. 당연한 결과지.

expressions.dialogue:
Joey: All right, you're probably not gonna want to hear this but, if it was me, I would ah, **I would bow out.**
Chandler: What? What are you talking about?
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.nuance: 억지로 밀려나는 게 아니라 품위 있게 스스로 물러나는 것. 연인 관계, 경쟁, 비즈니스 등 다양한 상황에서 쓸 수 있으며 긍정적인 뉘앙스를 담고 있다.
expressions.example: She clearly likes him more, so I decided to bow out gracefully. / 그녀가 분명히 그를 더 좋아하니까, 나는 깔끔하게 물러나기로 했어.

expressions.dialogue: Joey: There's a chance they could make that work, I know I wouldn't want to be the guy who **stood in the way of that.**
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.nuance: 어떤 관계나 목표가 이루어지는 것을 막는 사람이나 요인을 가리킬 때 사용. 'Don't let anything stand in the way of your dreams'처럼 긍정적 목표에도 쓸 수 있다.
expressions.example: I won't stand in the way of your happiness. / 나는 네 행복을 방해하지 않을 거야.

occ.speaker: Danny
occ.text: Okay, listen, I really like you. Okay? I think this can go somewhere. So what if I'm close to my family, are you gonna let that stand in the way of us?

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: That wouldn't stand in the way of a true pianist.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: I don't wanna stand in the way of true love or anything, but I think a cantaloupe might hurt less.

expressions.expression: I don't wanna be the guy who...
expressions.dialogue: Chandler: **I don't wanna be the guy that breaks up a family**, y'know when my parents split up, it was because of that guy.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: ~하는 사람이 되고 싶지 않다 (특정 역할이나 행동을 거부할 때)
expressions.nuance: 도덕적 이유나 자기 이미지 때문에 특정 행동을 거부할 때 쓰는 패턴. 자기 합리화나 설득의 맥락에서 쓰이며 상대방의 공감을 이끌어내는 효과가 있다.
expressions.example: I don't wanna be the guy who shows up late to everything. / 항상 늦게 나타나는 사람이 되고 싶지 않아.

expressions.dialogue: Phoebe: Just **be on the look out** for anything that you can fall into, or that can fall on you... All right, just look out!
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.nuance: 위험하거나 특정 상황을 주의 깊게 찾아볼 때 쓰는 표현. 경찰 방송이나 뉴스에서도 자주 등장하며, 일상 대화에서도 자연스럽게 쓸 수 있다.
expressions.example: Be on the lookout for any signs of water damage after the storm. / 폭풍이 지나고 나면 물 피해 흔적이 있는지 주의 깊게 살펴봐.

expressions.dialogue: Ross: (noticing Ben's rain outfit) **What'd you do, take him whaling?**
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.meaning: 대체 뭘 한 거야? (예상치 못한 결과를 보고 놀라거나 빈정거릴 때)
expressions.nuance: 실제 대답을 기대하는 질문이 아니라 과장된 반응을 표현하는 수사적 질문. 뒤에 오는 행동이 상황과 어울리지 않을수록 유머 효과가 커진다.
expressions.example: What'd you do, run a marathon? You look completely wiped out. / 마라톤이라도 뛴 거야? 완전히 녹초가 된 것 같은데.

occ.speaker: Chan
occ.text: So what'd you do?

occ.speaker: Susan
occ.text: What'd you do, dip it in sugar?

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Yeesh, what'd you do about it?

expressions.dialogue: Ross: It was your first time babysitting, **I figured** you did the best you could.
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.meaning: 나는 ~라고 생각했어, 당연히 그럴 거라고 짐작했어
expressions.nuance: 'I thought'보다 더 구어적이고 캐주얼한 추론 표현. 논리적 근거 없이 직감이나 상황을 바탕으로 판단했을 때 자주 쓴다.
expressions.example: You seemed tired, so I figured you'd want to leave early. / 피곤해 보여서 일찍 가고 싶겠다고 생각했어.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Finally, I figure I'd better answer it, and it turns out it's my mother, which is very-very weird, because- she never calls me!

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: But see, it was a plan. Y'know, it was clear. It was figured out, and now everything's just kinda like...

occ.speaker: Janice
occ.text: Well, I knew you had the Rockys, and so I figured, you know, you can wear Bullwinkle and Bullwinkle, or you can wear Rocky and Rocky,or, you can mix and match, moose and squirrel. Whatever you want.

expressions.dialogue: Joey: Okay, we're approaching the window. **Thread the needle. Thread the needle.**
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 좁은 공간을 정밀하게 통과하다; (비유적으로) 까다로운 상황을 조심스럽게 헤쳐나가다
expressions.nuance: 원래는 바늘귀에 실을 꿰는 행위에서 온 표현. 물리적으로 좁은 곳을 통과할 때도, 민감한 협상이나 복잡한 상황을 정교하게 처리할 때도 쓴다.
expressions.example: The diplomat had to thread the needle between two conflicting demands. / 외교관은 두 상충하는 요구 사이에서 아슬아슬하게 줄타기를 해야 했다.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Toe the line. Thread the needle. Think outside the box! (Tries to leave, but is stopped by Rachel.)

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Toe the line. Thread the needle. Think outside the box! (Tries to leave, but is stopped by Rachel.)

expressions.dialogue:
Ross: Thanks, Gunther. Can I get a napkin too?
Gunther: **Oh, like you don't already have everything.**
expressions.speaker: Gunther
expressions.meaning: 마치 너는 이미 다 가진 것처럼, 뻔하지 않아? (질투나 빈정거림을 담은 표현)
expressions.nuance: 상대방이 이미 충분히 갖고 있거나 누리고 있는데도 더 원할 때 비꼬는 어조로 쓰는 표현. 가벼운 장난부터 진지한 불만 표출까지 상황에 따라 강도가 달라진다.
expressions.example: Oh, like you don't already know the answer. / 마치 이미 답을 모르는 것처럼 굴지 마.

expressions.dialogue: Chandler: I have something I need to tell you, and **I want you to let me get through it**, because it's not gonna be easy.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 끝까지 말하게 해줘, 중간에 끊지 말고 다 들어줘
expressions.nuance: 'Get through something'은 힘든 것을 버텨내거나 완수하다는 의미. 감정적으로 어려운 이야기를 전달할 때 끊기지 않게 해달라고 부탁하는 표현이다.
expressions.example: Just let me get through my apology without interrupting, please. / 제발 끼어들지 말고 내 사과 다 들어줘.

expressions.dialogue: Monica: **Oh my God, I wrecked your baby!!** (runs into the bedroom)
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: 망가뜨리다, 완전히 못쓰게 만들다 (신체적·감정적 모두 가능)
expressions.nuance: 'Ruin'보다 더 강렬하고 즉각적인 파괴의 뉘앙스. 감정적으로도 'You wrecked me'(네가 나를 무너뜨렸어)처럼 쓸 수 있으며, 충격과 자책감이 클 때 어울린다.
expressions.example: The breakup completely wrecked him for months. / 그 이별이 몇 달 동안 그를 완전히 무너뜨렸어.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Look, Ross, you have what you want, you're back with Rachel. If you bring this up now you're gonna wreck the best thing that even happened to you.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: No! You don't want to wreck it, you don't want to go to fast!

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Terrible. If-if I want something done right, I have to do it myself. Other people just wreck stuff. I really think I might kill someone tonight.

expressions.dialogue:
Chandler: Then don't leave me!
Janice: What?
Chandler: Forget what I said, **I was babbling!** Pick me!
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.nuance: 자신이 한 말이 논리 없이 마구 나온 것임을 인정하거나 취소할 때 사용. 당황하거나 감정이 앞서서 내뱉은 말을 수습하려 할 때 자주 쓴다.
expressions.example: Sorry, I was just babbling — ignore what I said. / 미안, 그냥 횡설수설한 거야 — 방금 한 말은 무시해.

expressions.dialogue: Rachel: Phoebe, you're in pain, **would you just go to the dentist, just go.**
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.nuance: 상대방이 당연히 해야 할 일을 하지 않을 때 짜증과 걱정이 섞인 어조로 재촉하는 표현. 뒤에 'just'를 반복하면 강도가 높아진다.
expressions.example: Would you just tell me the truth already? / 제발 그냥 솔직하게 말해줄래?

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Do you think things would have been better if you'd just told her the truth?

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: (gives her an envelope) Would you just open it?

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Yes. Yes. Yes! Yes, I really do! In fact, it's funny, very often, someone who you wouldn't think could-could curl your toes, might just be the one who...

expressions.dialogue: Ross: You didn't kill anybody, these people **just happened to die** when you went to the dentist. It's just a coincidence.
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.nuance: 어떤 사건이 우연의 일치임을 강조할 때 사용. 때로는 의도치 않은 일을 변명하거나 대수롭지 않게 넘기려 할 때도 쓴다. 긍정·부정 상황 모두 가능하다.
expressions.example: I just happened to run into my ex at the airport. / 공항에서 우연히 전 남자친구를 만났어.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Oh, hi, Ross. Yeah. There's someone I want you to say hi to. (to Chandler) He just happened to call.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: There just don't happen to be any women in our games.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Okay, how about the fact that he's engaged to another woman, who just happens to be your ex-best friend?

expressions.dialogue:
Monica: Besides, **it takes the focus off the hat.**
Rachel: Right.
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.nuance: 불편하거나 숨기고 싶은 것을 다른 것으로 주의를 돌릴 때 사용. 패션, 대화, 마케팅 등 다양한 상황에서 쓸 수 있다.
expressions.example: Add a bold necklace — it takes the focus off the stain on your shirt. / 눈에 띄는 목걸이를 해봐 — 셔츠의 얼룩에서 시선이 분산될 거야.

expressions.dialogue:
Janice: I've got to give my marriage another chance.
Chandler: No you don't! I say you have to **give your divorce another chance.**
expressions.speaker: Chandler (반전 버전)
expressions.nuance: 포기했던 것이나 관계를 다시 시도해볼 때 쓰는 표현. Chandler처럼 기존 표현의 단어를 바꿔 비틀면 유머나 반전 효과를 낼 수 있다.
expressions.example: I know you had a rough time there, but give it another chance. / 거기서 힘들었던 거 알지만, 다시 한 번 기회를 줘봐.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Rach, come on, open up. Rach, come on, come on, Rach. You got to give me another chance.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Well, I think you should seriously consider the marriage thing, give Rachel another chance to dress up like Princess Bubble Yum.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Come on you guys, come on please-please just give her another chance, huh? She'll come around I promise.

expressions.dialogue:
Joey: Oh God. Uh, okay, here's the thing, this is the thing, okay, **the thing is...**
Chandler: What is the thing?
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 있잖아, 사실은 말이야... (본론을 꺼내기 전에 뜸을 들이거나 망설일 때)
expressions.nuance: 말하기 불편하거나 복잡한 이야기를 꺼낼 때 쓰는 대화 도입 표현. 종종 나쁜 소식이나 반박이 뒤따르는 신호가 되기도 한다. 'Here's the thing'으로도 많이 쓴다.
expressions.example: I know you want me to come, but the thing is, I already made other plans. / 오길 원하는 거 알지만, 사실 이미 다른 약속이 있어.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Alright, so I'm back in high school, I'm standing in the middle of the cafeteria, and I realize I am totally naked.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Marsha, these are cave people. Okay? They have issues like 'Gee, that glacier's getting kinda close.' See?

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Can you believe it? ...Y'know what? I just don't feel the thing. I mean, they feel the thing, I don't feel the thing.

expressions.dialogue: Ross: He's leaving out 'e' and 'f.' It's like they just, I don't know, **fell out of his head.**
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.nuance: 알고 있던 것이 갑자기 생각나지 않거나 사라진 것처럼 느껴질 때 쓰는 구어적이고 시각적인 표현. 유머러스하거나 당황스러운 상황에 어울린다.
expressions.example: I studied all night, but everything fell out of my head during the exam. / 밤새 공부했는데 시험 중에 다 머릿속에서 빠져나갔어.

expressions.dialogue: Monica: It's no big deal, **it's not even worth mentioning**, you see we all do it all the time.
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.nuance: 진짜로 사소한 일을 가볍게 넘길 때도 쓰지만, Monica처럼 실제로는 중요한 일을 의도적으로 작게 만들려 할 때도 사용된다. 맥락을 파악하는 게 중요하다.
expressions.example: It's just a tiny scratch — not worth mentioning. / 그냥 아주 작은 흠집이야 — 언급할 것도 없어.

expressions.dialogue:
Joey: Girls love gum.
Chandler: That's a good idea, 'Dear Janice have a Hubba-Bubba birthday'. I would like to get her **something serious.**
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 진지한 것, 의미 있는 것 (단순하거나 가벼운 것의 반대)
expressions.nuance: 선물이나 행동이 가볍지 않고 감정이나 진심이 담긴 것을 원할 때 사용. 관계의 진지함을 암시하는 표현으로도 쓰인다.
expressions.example: I don't want to just send flowers — I want to do something serious to show how much I care. / 꽃만 보내기는 싫어 — 얼마나 신경 쓰는지 보여줄 의미 있는 일을 하고 싶어.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Seriously, what-what's the part?

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: You're not serious, I mean, you really... you really talk to it?

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Wow, this is serious. I've never known you to pay money for any kind of capade.

expressions.dialogue:
Joey: Hey-hey, Ross?
Ross: Yeah.
Joey: **I've got a science question.**
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.nuance: 'I have a question'보다 더 구어적이고 캐주얼한 표현. 공식적으로 제기하기보다 자연스럽게 말을 꺼낼 때 어울리며, 친근한 어투를 만들어준다.
expressions.example: Hey, I've got a quick question about tonight's plan. / 저기, 오늘 밤 계획에 대해 잠깐 물어볼 게 있어.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: I have no idea.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: The problem is, though, after the concert's over, no matter how great the show was, you girls are always looking for the comedian again, y'know? I mean, we're in the car, we're fighting traffic... basically just trying to stay awake.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: My grandmother has this new boyfriend, and they're both kind of insecure in bed. Oh, and deaf. So they're constantly, like, having to reassure each other that they're having a good time. You have no idea how loud they are!

expressions.expression: I have a lot of experience in that area
expressions.dialogue: Joey: Something this big and long is going to be difficult to maneuver, fortunately **I have a lot of experience in that area.**
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 그 분야에 경험이 많다 (맥락에 따라 이중적 의미로도 쓰임)
expressions.nuance: 'In that area'는 '그 분야에서', '그 부분에 있어서'라는 뜻의 자연스러운 표현. Joey처럼 맥락에 따라 이중적 의미를 품기도 하지만, 일반적으로는 특정 분야의 경험이나 전문성을 나타낼 때 쓴다.
expressions.example: She's the best person to ask — she has a lot of experience in that area. / 그녀에게 물어보는 게 최선이야 — 그 분야에 경험이 많거든.
